children.text: Afterpay is a buy now, pay later payment method offering shoppers the option to pay in four interest-free installments. Originally from Australia, Afterpay is now also available in New Zealand, the US, and Canada, as well as the UK where they operate under the brand Clearpay.

api_fields.value: Afterpay Touch Group, Afterpay Limited, AFTERPAY LIMITED

excerpt: BNPL that lets shoppers split purchases into four interest-free installments; called Clearpay in the UK.

content_features.title: Buy now, pay later in 4 installments
content_features.description: Offer shoppers the option to pay over four interest-free installments at checkout.

content_features.title: Regional branding (Afterpay vs Clearpay)
content_features.description: Use Afterpay in AU/NZ/US/CA and Clearpay in the UK to match local shopper expectations.

content_features.title: Online checkout flow with redirect
content_features.description: Typically completed via an online checkout flow where the shopper is redirected to complete the payment and then returned.

content_features.title: Coverage across key markets
content_features.description: Supports Australia, New Zealand, the United States, Canada, and the United Kingdom (Clearpay).

content_features.title: Authorization success depends on data quality
content_features.description: Approval rates can be sensitive to checkout data accuracy (name, address, email, phone) and order details.

content_features.title: Refund support
content_features.description: Supports refunds (including partial refunds where enabled by your PSP/integration).

content_features.title: Disputes and chargebacks may apply
content_features.description: As with other consumer payment methods, disputes/chargebacks can occur and should be handled via your PSP’s dispute workflow.

content_features.title: Multi-currency settlement
content_features.description: Afterpay & Clearpay supports settlement across multiple currencies depending on partner or acquirer setup.

tip: If you see lower approval rates, review the shopper identity and address fields you send at checkout, especially email, phone, billing, and shipping details.

faq.question: What is the difference between Afterpay and Clearpay?
faq.answer: They are the same BNPL product under different brands: Afterpay in AU/NZ/US/CA and Clearpay in the UK.

faq.question: Where is Afterpay/Clearpay available?
faq.answer: Common availability includes Australia, New Zealand, the United States, Canada, and the United Kingdom (as Clearpay). Availability can vary by PSP and merchant setup.

faq.question: Why are some Afterpay/Clearpay payments declined?
faq.answer: Declines can happen due to shopper eligibility checks and inconsistent or missing checkout data (such as email, phone, or address details). Improving data completeness often helps authorization success.

faq.question: Is Afterpay/Clearpay a PSP or an acquirer?
faq.answer: Afterpay/Clearpay is a payment method/BNPL provider. Merchants typically connect through a PSP (for example, via an integrated payment platform) rather than integrating it as a card acquirer.

faq.question: Does Afterpay or Clearpay support multiple currencies?
faq.answer: Shopper-facing currency support depends on market and PSP setup, while settlement currency options can vary by region and integration partner.

seo.seo_description: Afterpay (Clearpay in the UK) is a BNPL payment method that lets shoppers pay in four interest-free installments. Available in AU, NZ, US, CA and UK.
seo.keywords: Afterpay, Clearpay, BNPL, buy now pay later, installments
